citation_label: Nigeria Tax Act, 2025 (2025 No. 7) — sections 30 and 58 and Fourth Schedule slices
heading: Nigeria Tax Act, 2025 (2025 No. 7) — sections 30 and 58 and Fourth Schedule slices

metadata.commencement_note: The Act's own text has no commencement provision (section 203 is citation-only) and section 196 repeals the Personal Income Tax Act, Cap. P8 LFN 2004 "from the commencement of this Act". The announced nationwide implementation date for the 2025 tax reform acts is 1 January 2026, and individual income tax operates by calendar year of assessment (section 202), so 2026 is the first year of assessment under the Fourth Schedule rates.
metadata.gazette_reference: Official Gazette No. 117, Vol. 112, 26 June 2025, Government Notice No. 26, pages A385-A597
metadata.hosting_note: Text-embedded PDF of the gazette print downloaded from the Gombe State Internal Revenue Service (irs.gm.gov.ng), a Nigerian state tax authority hosting the federal gazette act, on 2026-07-06. The same gazette print circulates from the Presidential Fiscal Policy and Tax Reforms Committee (fiscalreforms.ng) and the Chartered Institute of Taxation of Nigeria (portal.citn.org).
metadata.source_authority: Federal Republic of Nigeria Official Gazette (Federal Government Printer, Lagos); hosted by the Gombe State Internal Revenue Service
metadata.source_note: Slices promoted at section/schedule granularity via page_windows: section 30 (page 29 of the PDF, gazette A413), section 58 (page 41, gazette A425) and the Fourth Schedule (page 155, gazette A539). Rate table verbatim: First N800,000 at 0%; Next N2,200,000 at 15%; Next N9,000,000 at 18%; Next N13,000,000 at 21%; Next N25,000,000 at 23%; Above N50,000,000 at 25%. Band widths are internally consistent with the cumulative boundaries (800,000 / 3,000,000 / 12,000,000 / 25,000,000 / 50,000,000).
metadata.supersedes_note: Replaces the Personal Income Tax Act regime (graduated rates and consolidated relief allowance) previously modelled by PolicyEngine Nigeria; the consolidated relief allowance is abolished and rent relief (section 30(2)(a)(vi)) replaces it as the general relief for individuals.
metadata.title: Nigeria Tax Act, 2025 (2025 No. 7) — sections 30 and 58 and Fourth Schedule slices

body: that individual ascertained under the provisions of section 28 of this Act, less eligible deductions. (2) For the purposes of this section – (a) "eligible deductions" include payments made by the individual in a year of assessment in respect of – (i) the individual's contributions under the National Housing Fund, (ii) the individual's contributions under the National Health Insurance Scheme, (iii) the individual's contributions under the Pension Reform Act, (iv) interest on loans for developing an owner-occupied residential house, (v) annual amount of any annuity or premium paid by the individual during the year preceding the year of assessment in respect of insurance on his life or the life of his spouse, or contract for a deferred annuity on his own life or the life of his spouse; and (vi) rent relief of 20% of annual rent paid, subject to a maximum of N500,000, whichever is lower, provided that the individual accurately declares the actual amount of rent paid and other relevant information as may be prescribed by the relevant tax authority ; and (b) "total income" means total income as specified in section 28 of this Act.

body: Section 58(1) INDIVIDUALS' INCOME TAX RATES After the relief allowance and exemptions had been granted in accordance with subsection (1) of section 30 of this Act, the taxable income ascertained shall be taxed at the following rates – (a) First N800,000 at 0%; (b) Next N2,200,000 at 15%; (c) Next N9,000,000 at 18%; (d) Next N13,000,000 at 21%; (e) Next N25,000,000 at 23%; and (f) Above N50,000,000 at 25%.
